# COMS4419_FInal_Project Author: Hanyu Xie hx2413, Haotian Lei hl3945

When Trends Become Policy: Measuring the Influence of TikTok and Weibo on Consumer Goods and Regulation

The project we have discussed will mainly rely on data analysis, we will collect supportive data, construct our own data set, visualize and interpret and analyze. We might need to train a small model utilizing our collected data to do some forecast, pack it just for fun. 

We will primarily use Jupyter notebook to do analysis, and utilize statistical methods to indicate correlations between trend and effect. We will find a way to quantify outcomes. We will do a google survey if necessary to collect some customer data, and interview if needed.

This project studies highly regulated social media platforms such as Tiktok and Weibo, and asks how large online trends translate into real-world market shifts and policy responses. Compared with the U.S., China’s media environment operates under much stronger government rules and intervention, which makes the dynamics of “what becomes a trend” meaningfully different. While some topics and narratives can be guided or constrained, public opinion is not fully controllable; the government can shape what gets amplified, but it cannot fully control what people think or how they react.
In practice, unexpected events still explode online. A single clip, rumor, or incident can snowball into a massive trend, and public sentiment may keep intensifying until it becomes impossible for officials and brands to ignore. When that happens, the spillover is often visible: agencies respond with statements, investigations, new guidelines, or even formal regulations, and consumer goods markets can shift at the same time through price swings, demand spikes, or sudden changes in brand reputation. By combining platform trend signals with market and policy timelines, the project aims to quantify how strong these effects are, and when they are most likely to happen.

Haotian: Collect and organize social media trend data from TikTok and Weibo. Construct datasets and perform basic quantitative data analysis and visualization. Analyze the timing and relationships between online trends and market or policy responses, in collaboration with Hanyu. Collaborate in mainscript writing and presentation preparation.
Hanyu: Collaborate in data collection, analysis, and visualizations. Research about policy regulation during designated timeline, study the effect based on media trends. Find a way to quantify outcomes. Do modeling, survey collection, interview if needed. Collaborate in mainscript writing and presentation preparation.
